rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT CHARLIE SETO'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO 1) COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND 2) COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO FILE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR THE COURT TO ORDER DISMISSAL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT CHARLIE SETO'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO 1) COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND 2) COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO FILE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR THE COURT TO ORDER DISMISSAL is GRANTED. Plaintiff is in breach of the License Agreement and Settlement of Claims, paragraph 10, which unequivocally required Plaintiff to dismiss the action with prejudice within 10 days of execution of the Agreement. Plaintiff shall prepare a form of dismissal, present a hard copy to the Court for review at the hearing, and be prepared to file it electronically by noon on January 31, 2022. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, executed by the parties, the Court retains jurisdiction to enforce the Agreement, which includes a binding Arbitration clause covering "any dispute between the parties arising from this Agreement of performance under it." Any such dispute (including the dispute over the termination of the license or existence of an easement claimed in this action) shall proceed in Arbitration. The Court will thereafter hold a hearing and enter a final judgment on any motion to confirm/vacate the arbitration award per the terms of the Agreement. Supplemental Opposition and Reply are stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant Zhijun Wus Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2023 line 3. CROSS-DEFENDANT ZHIJUN WU'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is continued to February 24, 2022 for opposing party to comply with SFLR 2.7B and Court's prior minute order to provide courtesy copies. rulings.Case Title: RYDER-POST STREET LLC VS. JESSE POWELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT ADAM WILLIAMS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry his burden. Proof of Service by a registered process server indicates that Defendants were sub-served on a person described in the Proof of Service. In the motion defendant Williams asserts that he doesn't match the description of the person served. Based on the proof of service, Plaintiff does not claim that Mr. Williams was personally served. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: T. & A. DROLAPAS & SONS, L.P. VS. PAULA LAWS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT PAULA LAWS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is DENIED. Moving party fails to sustain her burden under CCP 473(b). Defendant was served on November 14, 2022; default was entered on December 13, 2022 (one month after service); Defendant sought relief on January 24, 2023 (over two months after service and 6 weeks after entry of default). Defendant's declaration (without corroborating evidence) accounts for 2 weeks of this time. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
